title: PCSK9 LOF carriers → drug MoA inference
description: Given a brief evidence sheet on PCSK9 loss-of-function carriers (lower LDL-C, lower CHD risk, no adverse phenotype), infer which mechanism of action a PCSK9-targeted drug should adopt. The expected answer is 'inhibitor': mimicking the protective LOF state with a drug means decreasing PCSK9 activity. This is the canonical genetics-to-MoA inference that motivated alirocumab and evolocumab; the file deliberately does NOT name those drugs.
task_prompt: Read the file genetic_evidence.txt in your working directory. Based on the population-genetics findings described, choose exactly one of the three MoA strategies. Write your answer as JSON to eval_answer.json with this exact schema: {"moa": "<value>"}, where <value> must be the literal string "agonist", "inhibitor", or "unclear" — written in full, lowercase, no other words and no abbreviations. Allowed values: "agonist", "inhibitor", "unclear".

inputs.description: Brief on PCSK9 LOF human-genetics findings

notes: The reasoning chain: LOF carriers have lower LDL-C and lower CHD risk with no adverse phenotype -> reducing PCSK9 activity is therapeutically desirable -> the drug should reduce PCSK9 activity -> inhibitor. The evidence file deliberately names variants but not drugs, so an answer driven by prior knowledge of alirocumab/evolocumab cannot bypass the inference.
